_note: Per-axis selection tunables (Plans A-E). Hot-editable; read with a robust fallback to built-in defaults so a malformed edit never breaks the pipeline. Implemented: arc_completeness (A), reaction (B). See AIclippingPipelineVault/wiki/concepts/clipping-quality-overhaul.md.

global._note: Cross-axis compounding guardrail (overhaul eval finding #1). The A-E axis multipliers each stay bounded, but their PRODUCT is clamped to [floor, ceil] before being applied to raw_score so no moment runs away by tripping several correlated axes at once. Only arc may push below 1.0; the rest are boost-only.

reaction._note: Plan B — reaction-worthy. Cheap INTENSITY pre-signal (audio crowd-response near the peak + a post-beat chat-breadth spike over [T, T+post_window_s]). Boost-only and deliberately the SMALLEST ceiling, since energy is already heavily rewarded in Pass C (cross_validated x1.20, speaker x1.15, Pass A crowd gating) — B must not amplify energy bias. Authenticity (earned vs forced hype) is the Vision Judge's job, not measured here.

baseline_contrast._note: Plan C — baseline-contrast (the most novel axis + the corrective for energy bias). Compute the streamer's per-VOD 'normal' once, then BOOST moments that break it: two-sided speaking-rate z-deviation (fast AND slow are breaks), a start-aligned topic pivot (NOT a mid-clip crossing, so it doesn't fight Plan A), and a genre shift (an unusual segment-type for this VOD). Boost-only; given the MOST authority (ceil 1.18). Orthogonal to the M1 speaker boost (no double-count). Cold-start guarded.

arc_completeness._note: Structural 'is this a complete, self-contained setup->payoff arc?' score in [0,1] -> a gentle, category-aware multiplier on Pass C raw_score. Boost-leaning, never gates. neutral_point is the completeness at which the multiplier is exactly 1.0; below it = mild penalty, above = mild boost.
